creator: cem@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to Fabian Keil from comment #9)
> While dd keeps a core busy as expected, rand_harvestq stays below 3%
> cpu use according to top.
> ...
> 
> The fact that rand_harvestq is busy doesn't necessarily indicate that
> the entropy pool is starved.

True.  On 12.0 through head, if you have concurrent random readers (especially 2+), rand_harvestq CPU time may just indicate it is spinning on the global Fortuna lock while a reader generates random data.  Some more detail in my earlier comment #10.
